stories.title: Vevor Baby Swing Recall — Suffocation Hazard (CPSC)

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text: RECALL: Vevor baby swings (BB501K, BB702A, BB005K) recalled for suffocation risk — violates the inclined sleeper ban. Stop use immediately. Refund: cut cover in half, email recalling@vevor.com. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text: About 1,020 Vevor baby swings sold on Amazon (Jan–Aug 2025, $65–$80) recalled. Inclined sleepers cause suffocation risk. Families: destroy cover, photo it, email recalling@vevor.com for full refund. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

article.headline: Vevor Baby Swing Recall: What Colorado Springs Families Need to Do Now

image_concepts.label: Image
image_concepts.raw:
Vevor Baby Swing Recall

**Image 1-A** (Social 1200x675)
- **Mode:** base_only
- **Prompt:** A clean, minimalist safety warning graphic. Warm but serious red-orange gradient background. A baby swing silhouette with a clear red X symbol over it. Bold white text space at bottom: "SAFETY RECALL". Simple, immediate, alarming but not terrifying. No text baked in. Studio lighting quality, flat lay style.
- **Usage:** Primary social post for S1-A and S1-B
- **Notes:** COS Parenting brand colors (Teal #0097A7, Coral #FF6F61); safety/urgent tone

**Image 1-B** (Article Hero 1200x675)
- **Mode:** base_only
- **Prompt:** Overhead view of a parent looking at a product label, warm natural lighting, living room setting. Soft focus on baby items in the background. Conveys careful product safety checking. Diverse representation — could be any family. No text, no specific product branding visible.
- **Usage:** Article hero for article-01
- **Notes:** COS Parenting: authentic, warm, practical tone

---

research.facts:
Vevor Baby Swing Recall
**Source 1:** CPSC official (cpsc.gov/Recalls/2026/Sanven-Technology-Recalls-Vevor-Baby-Swings...)
- Sanven Technology recalls Vevor baby swings
- Violation: mandatory standard for Infant Sleep Products + inclined sleepers ban
- ~1,020 units; sold Jan–Aug 2025 on vevor.com and Amazon; $65–$80
- Models: BB501K, BB702A, BB005K
- **Confidence: HIGH**
**Source 2:** Motherly (mother.ly/parenting/safety/vevor-baby-swing-recall/)
- "Parents urged to stop using these Vevor baby swings after federal safety recall"
- Incline angle >10 degrees; head-forward suffocation mechanism confirmed
- Remedy: destroy cover, email recalling@vevor.com; call 855-599-6320 Mon–Fri 9 AM–5 PM PT
- **Confidence: HIGH**
**Source 3:** vevor.com/pages/vevor-baby-swing-recall (brand recall page)
- Full refund available; destroy product before refund
- **Confidence: HIGH**
**Verified Facts (STORY 1):**
- [HIGH] ~1,020 units recalled
- [HIGH] Sold Jan–Aug 2025 on vevor.com and Amazon; price $65–$80
- [HIGH] Models: BB501K, BB702A, BB005K
- [HIGH] Violation: Safe Sleep for Babies Act (inclined sleeper ban)
- [HIGH] Hazard: suffocation from incline >10 degrees
- [HIGH] Remedy: full refund; contact recalling@vevor.com or 855-599-6320 (M–F, 9 AM–5 PM PT)

stories.title: COS Parks to Close 25% of Restrooms and Remove Playground Equipment — Budget Cuts

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text: Colorado Springs families: ~25% of neighborhood park restrooms will be closed all summer due to budget cuts. Damaged playground equipment will also be removed, not repaired. Check before your next park day. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text: COS parks this summer: 10 neighborhood restrooms closed, playground equipment removed instead of replaced, Julie Penrose Fountain maintenance halted. Know before you go. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

article.headline: COS Parks Budget Cuts: What Families Should Know Before Summer 2026

image_concepts.label: Image
image_concepts.raw:
COS Parks Budget Cuts

**Image 2-A** (Social 1200x675)
- **Mode:** base_only
- **Prompt:** A wide park scene with an empty swing set, a "Closed" sign on a restroom building, and a warm afternoon sky in the background. Colorado mountains visible in the distance. Natural colors, slight melancholy but not alarming. Realistic photography style. Open text area at bottom.
- **Usage:** Primary social posts S2-A and S2-B
- **Notes:** Local feel; COS Rocky Mountain backdrop

**Image 2-B** (Article Hero 1200x675)
- **Mode:** base_only
- **Prompt:** A family — adults and children — approaching a park gate on a sunny day. One adult notices a posted notice/sign. Children look up curiously. Warm light, Colorado mountains background. Realistic family scene. Diverse representation.
- **Usage:** Article hero for article-02

---

research.facts:
COS Parks Budget Cuts
**Source 1:** KRDO (krdo.com/news/2026/02/12/...)
- "What Colorado Springs City budget cuts could mean for your family this summer"
- Parks, Recreation and Cultural Services Advisory Board confirmed cuts
- ~25% (10) of neighborhood park permanent restrooms closed for 2026 season
- Playground equipment: damaged/vandalized equipment will be removed, not replaced
- Julie Penrose Fountain at America the Beautiful Park: no cleaning/restoration 2026, 2027, 2029, 2030
- **Confidence: HIGH**
**Source 2:** City of Colorado Springs (coloradosprings.gov/recreation, coloradosprings.gov/parks)
- PRCS operates 3 community centers, 7 sports complexes, 5 program areas
- Serves 300,000+ COS residents
- **Confidence: HIGH**
**Verified Facts (STORY 2):**
- [HIGH] ~25% (10) of neighborhood park permanent restrooms closed 2026 season
- [HIGH] Playground equipment: removal rather than repair/replacement
- [HIGH] Julie Penrose Fountain: halted cleaning/restoration 2026, 2027, 2029, 2030
- [HIGH] Source: Parks, Recreation and Cultural Services Advisory Board (KRDO Feb 2026)
- [MEDIUM] Specific list of which parks/restrooms are closed (not confirmed; general stat only)

research.facts:
D49 Graduation 2026

- Banning Lewis Preparatory Academy: May 19, 3:00 PM, BLPA Stadium

- Liberty Tree Academy: May 22, 6:00 PM
- Sand Creek High School: May 23, 9:00 AM, Broadmoor World Arena

- Palmer HS: May 19, 9:00 AM, Ed Robson Arena, Colorado College
- Mitchell HS: May 19, 3:00 PM, Ed Robson Arena
- Coronado HS: May 20, 9:00 AM, Ed Robson Arena
- Doherty HS: May 20, 3:00 PM, Ed Robson Arena
- Achieve/Tesla/Bijou/Odyssey: May 21
- **Confidence: HIGH**
**Verified Facts (STORY 3):**
- [HIGH] D49 graduation dates: May 15, 19, 22, 23 — venues as above
- [HIGH] D11 graduation dates: May 19-21 at Ed Robson Arena, Colorado College
- [MEDIUM] Parking/traffic specifics for Broadmoor World Arena (not separately confirmed)

stories.title: AAP 2026 Car Seat Safety — Rear-Facing as Long as Possible

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text: Updated AAP guidance (Feb 2026): keep children rear-facing until the car seat manufacturer's max height OR weight — not age. Summer road trips are coming. Is your COS family's setup up to date? #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

article.headline: AAP 2026 Car Seat Update: Keep Children Rear-Facing Longer Than You Think

image_concepts.label: Image
image_concepts.raw:
AAP Car Seat Safety

**Image 4-A** (Social 1200x675)
- **Mode:** base_only
- **Prompt:** A rear-facing infant car seat properly installed in a vehicle back seat. Clean, well-lit interior. A small plush toy next to the seat suggests a baby's space. No child in seat (safety imagery). Bright, reassuring light.
- **Usage:** Primary social post S4-A
- **Notes:** Practical, safety-focused

**Image 4-B** (Article Hero 1200x675)
- **Mode:** base_only
- **Prompt:** A parent carefully securing a child into a car seat, checking the harness. The child is smiling and looking up at the parent. Warm interior light. Authentic, loving moment. Side view showing rear-facing orientation clearly.
- **Usage:** Article hero for article-04

---

research.facts:
AAP Car Seat Safety 2026
**Source 1:** AAP (aap.org/en/policy/) and search results confirming update
- Child passenger safety guidance updated Feb 12, 2026
- Rear-facing: as long as possible until max height/weight per car seat manufacturer
- Updated guidance moves away from age-based milestones
- **Confidence: HIGH**
**Source 2:** Multiple AAP policy pages and healthychildren.org (standard AAP dissemination channel)
- Next stage: forward-facing with harness
- Following stage: high-back booster or booster with seatbelt
- **Confidence: HIGH**
**Verified Facts (STORY 4):**
- [HIGH] AAP car seat safety guidance updated Feb 12, 2026
- [HIGH] Rear-facing: until max height/weight dictated by car seat manufacturer
- [HIGH] Sequence: rear-facing → forward-facing with harness → booster → seat belt only
- [MEDIUM] Exact AAP document title — confirmed updated Feb 12, 2026 but exact policy title not confirmed in two sources

stories.title: May Is National Bike Month — COS Trails Safety Guide for Families

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text: May is National Bike Month. Colorado Springs has 100+ miles of family trails — Pikes Peak Greenway, Bear Creek, Monument Valley Park. Check the helmet fits two fingers above the eyebrow and is CPSC certified. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

article.headline: National Bike Month: A Colorado Springs Family Safety and Trails Guide

image_concepts.label: Image
image_concepts.raw:
National Bike Month

**Image 5-A** (Social 1200x675)
- **Mode:** base_only
- **Prompt:** A family cycling together on a paved trail. Colorado mountains and blue sky in background. Two children wearing bright helmets riding alongside an adult. Sunny May afternoon light. Cheerful, active, safe. Flat text area at bottom.
- **Usage:** Primary social post S5-A
- **Notes:** COS outdoors feel; celebratory/active

**Image 5-B** (Article Hero 1200x675)
- **Mode:** base_only
- **Prompt:** A child putting on a bicycle helmet with an adult helping fit it. Wide smile. Colorful, warm light. Green park grass background, hint of mountains. Action-oriented but calm.
- **Usage:** Article hero for article-05

research.facts:
National Bike Month / COS Trails Safety
**Source 1:** League of American Bicyclists (bikeleague.org)
- May = National Bike Month (established annually)
- **Confidence: HIGH**
**Source 2:** AAP and CPSC general safety guidance
- Helmets recommended for all ages cycling
- CPSC: properly fitted helmet reduces head injury risk significantly (commonly cited as "up to 85%")
- Helmet standards: CPSC certified required for sale in US
- **Confidence: HIGH (CPSC standard), MEDIUM (85% stat — widely cited, recommend verifying in article)**
**Source 3:** City of Colorado Springs parks (coloradosprings.gov/parks)
- COS has extensive multi-use trail system
- Pikes Peak Greenway, Bear Creek Regional Park, Monument Valley Park are known family trails
- **Confidence: HIGH**
**Verified Facts (STORY 5):**
- [HIGH] May = National Bike Month
- [HIGH] Helmets are CPSC certified; required for sale in US
- [HIGH] COS has extensive multi-use trail system
- [MEDIUM] "Up to 85% reduction in head injury" — widely cited stat; note as MEDIUM, flag in article
- [MEDIUM] 100+ miles of multi-use trails in COS — general city reference, not separately verified today
